# SENDEX

> SENDEX (SEND Explorer) is a web application for exploring pre-clinical
> regulatory study data in SEND format (FDA SENDIG 3.1). The analysis
> engine auto-generates every insight that can be auto-generated from
> XPT study files; the UI is built for scientists doing daily analytical
> work at small N (non-rodent, N=3-5) where honest uncertainty
> communication matters more than hidden-complexity defaults.

The stack: XPT inputs → Python/FastAPI generator pipeline → React/TypeScript
viewer. Output is structured JSON per study under `backend/generated/<study>/`.

Every numeric threshold, test method, and scoring rule documented here is
auto-generated from the live code and regenerated on engine change.

## Methods

- [methods.md](methods.md): Analytical methods registry. Every statistical test (STAT-*), classification algorithm (CLASS-*), and scoring formula in the engine, with rationale, alternatives considered, and implementation locations. Spec-unaware — methods are referenced by stable IDs (e.g. STAT-01 Welch's t-test).

## Scientific logic

- [scientific-logic.md](scientific-logic.md): Auto-generated review doc showing the full pipeline applied to the PointCross reference study (rat, 13-week subchronic, 61 endpoint summaries → 10 detected syndromes). Every definition, threshold, and worked example reflects current code. Includes reviewer questions for scientific validation.

## Validation

- [validation/README.md](validation/README.md): Validation-suite design — two layers (engine output facts vs signal-detection verdicts against reference cards).
- [validation/summary.md](validation/summary.md): Current per-study dashboard — signal detection, design match, assertion outcomes.
- [validation/engine-output.md](validation/engine-output.md): Per-study facts read from generated JSON (study design, dose groups, finding classification counts, NOAEL/LOAEL, target organs).
- [validation/signal-detection.md](validation/signal-detection.md): Binary DETECTED/MISSED verdicts per injected/documented signal, with effect sizes.
- [validation/references/](validation/references/): One YAML reference card per study encoding ground truth (injected signals, expected design). Source citations trace to nSDRG, study report, or define.pdf.

## Optional

- [img/](img/): Application screenshots (histopathology panel, syndrome panel, main view).

## Conventions for AI consumers

- SENDEX targets **small-N preclinical studies**. Avoid advice that assumes N>30 or parametric-normality defaults.
- **SEND domain codes** (DM, EX, LB, BW, MI, MA, CL, OM, TK, TS, etc.) are standard per CDISC SENDIG 3.1.
- **Dose groups** are labeled by ascending dose level; control is dose 0.
- **Target organ identification**, **dose-response patterns**, **NOAEL/LOAEL**, and **reversibility verdicts** are the primary regulatory outputs.
- The engine prefers **robust/non-parametric methods**; the one load-bearing normality assumption is tracked as GAP-236.

## Not included

Private architecture, specs, research, and backlog live under `docs/_internal/`
and are not part of the public docs surface.
